meta.title: DeepLens — AI-Powered Code Documentation
meta.description: DeepLens deeply explores your codebase with AI agents and generates beautiful, comprehensive documentation. Run locally, keep your code private.

hero.title: Understand Any Codebase
hero.namingStory: Deep analysis through an AI lens — see your code with clarity.
hero.description: AI agents explore your code, build knowledge graphs, and generate beautiful docs — all running locally on your machine.

showcase.title: See It in Action
showcase.subtitle: Four phases, from raw code to rich documentation

tabs.label: Explore
tabs.title: AI Agent Explores Your Code
tabs.description: The explorer agent navigates your codebase like an experienced developer — reading files, tracing dependencies, and mapping the architecture into a structured knowledge graph.
highlights: Custom MCP tools for file access

tabs.label: Review
tabs.title: Human-in-the-Loop Outline Review
tabs.description: Review the generated outline before documentation begins. Add, remove, or reorder knowledge domains. Drag-and-drop editing ensures the documentation matches your vision.

tabs.label: Generate
tabs.title: Deep Writer Generates Docs
tabs.description: The writing agent creates rich, contextual documentation for each knowledge domain — with code examples, architecture diagrams, and cross-references.

tabs.label: Search
tabs.title: Ask Your Codebase Anything
tabs.description: Query your documented codebase with natural language. Fast keyword search for quick lookups, or deep semantic search powered by vector embeddings.

features.title: Why DeepLens?
features.subtitle: Built different from traditional documentation tools

items.title: Active Exploration
items.description: AI agent proactively explores your codebase using custom MCP tools — not just parsing, actually understanding.

items.title: Knowledge Graphs
items.description: Intelligent outline generation groups code into meaningful knowledge domains, not just file trees.

items.title: Dual-Layer Search
items.description: Fast keyword search for quick lookups, plus deep semantic search powered by vector embeddings.

items.title: MCP Integration
items.description: Works with any MCP-compatible client: Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code. Your docs become an AI knowledge base.

items.title: Incremental Updates
items.description: Only re-analyze changed files. Git-aware diffing keeps documentation fresh without full re-runs.

items.title: Bilingual Docs
items.description: Generate documentation in both English and Chinese simultaneously. Translation agent handles localization.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: From zero to documentation in four simple steps

steps.title: Install
steps.description: Download the desktop app, or install via Homebrew / npm.

steps.title: Select
steps.description: Open the DeepLens app and select your project directory.

steps.title: Explore
steps.description: AI agent deeply analyzes your code, building a knowledge graph of your architecture.

steps.title: Read
steps.description: Browse beautiful VitePress documentation with search, diagrams, and cross-references.

comparison.title: DeepLens vs DeepWiki
comparison.subtitle: Both generate documentation from code — but with different approaches
rows: 100% local — code never leaves your machine
Complete privacy, your API key only
Code sent to Cognition's servers for analysis
Agent-based exploration with custom MCP tools
Free & open source (bring your own API key)
Free for public repos, paid for private
Works offline (needs API only for analysis)

items.question: What is DeepLens?
items.answer: DeepLens is an open-source AI-powered code documentation tool. It uses AI agents to deeply explore your codebase and generate comprehensive, structured documentation — all running locally on your machine.

items.question: How is DeepLens different from DeepWiki?
items.answer: DeepWiki (deepwiki.com) is a cloud service by Cognition AI — your code is sent to their servers for analysis. DeepLens runs entirely on your machine, keeping code private. DeepLens also offers human-in-the-loop outline editing, VitePress static site output, and MCP server integration that DeepWiki doesn't provide.

items.question: Is DeepLens free and open source?
items.answer: Yes! DeepLens is fully open source under the MIT license. You'll need an API key for the AI model (Claude or OpenRouter), but the tool itself is free.

items.question: What programming languages are supported?
items.answer: DeepLens is language-agnostic — the AI agent reads and understands any programming language. It works best with TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, and other popular languages.

items.question: How does the AI agent work?
items.answer: DeepLens uses Claude's agent capabilities with custom MCP tools. The explorer agent reads files, lists directories, and searches patterns to build a knowledge graph. The generator agent then writes documentation based on this graph.

items.question: Can I customize the generated documentation?
items.answer: Absolutely. After the explorer agent produces an outline, you can review and edit it — add, remove, or reorder knowledge domains using a drag-and-drop editor before generation begins.

items.question: Does it work offline?
items.answer: DeepLens runs locally and stores all data on your machine. However, it requires an API connection to the AI model (Claude API or OpenRouter) for the analysis and generation steps.

cta.title: Ready to Understand Your Code?
cta.description: Start generating beautiful documentation for your codebase in minutes. Open source, privacy-first, and powered by AI.
